title: centaurea drabifolia subsp. cappadocica
intro: Centaurea drabifolia subsp. cappadocica, also known as Cappadocian knapweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Turkey and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Cappadocian knapweed include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antiviral properties. Traditional herbal preparations of Cappadocian knapweed include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate various health issues.
